articles.title: IMD weather alert: Only 90% rainfall in June, record-breaking heatwave in THESE states due to..., Delhi, Noida, Gurugram, UP, Bihar will...; check IMD monsoon forecast - news24online.com
articles.full_text:
IMD weather alert: Only 90% rainfall in June, record-breaking heatwave in THESE states due to…, Delhi, Noida, Gurugram, UP, Bihar will…; check IMD monsoon forecast
According to the IMD monsoon forecast, many parts of the country are likely to witness below-average rainfall, leading to record-breaking heatwaves in eight states including Delhi and nearby NCR districts of Noida, Ghaziabad, Gurugram, and Faridabad, as well as parts of Uttar Pradesh and Bihar.
According to the IMD monsoon forecast, many parts of the country are likely to witness below-average rainfall, leading to record-breaking heatwaves in eight states including Delhi and nearby NCR districts of Noida, Ghaziabad, Gurugram, and Faridabad, as well as parts of Uttar Pradesh and Bihar.
Below-average rainfall expected during southwest monsoon
As per the India Meteorological Department (IMD), the June-September southwest monsoon seasonal rainfall over India is expected to be 90 per cent of the long-period average with a model error of 4 percent. "The southwest monsoon seasonal rainfall over the monsoon core zone consisting of most of the rainfed agriculture areas in the country is most likely to be below normal (less than 94 pc of LPA)," Mrutyunjay Mohapatra, Director General of Meteorology at IMD said in a statement.
What is 'long-period average' rainfall?
The long-period average (LPA) refers to the rainfall recorded over a particular region for a given interval, such as a month or season, averaged over a long period of time, typically 30 to 50 years. The LPA of seasonal rainfall over the country as a whole, based on data from 1971 to 2020, is 87 cm. The average rainfall for the country as a whole in June is most likely to be below normal -- less than 92 pc of the LPA, Mohapatra said.
IMD warns of intense heatwave conditions in these states
As per the IMD monsoon forecast, above normal monthly maximum temperatures are likely over most parts of the country in June, including in parts of Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, Punjab, Bihar, Odisha, Chhattisgarh, Gujarat, and Andhra Pradesh and isolated regions of Maharashtra. However, below-normal heatwave days are likely over Rajasthan and Jharkhand, Mohapatra said.
When will monsoon arrive in Kerala?
According to the IMD chief, the onset of monsoon over Kerala is expected to occur in the next seven days. Typically, Kerala witnesses the onset of southwest monsoon around June 1, which marks the beginning of the southwest monsoon season in the country. The weather office said the northeast is likely to witness normal rainfall this monsoon season, while the remaining parts of the country may see below normal rainfall.
What does IMD's second monsoon forecast predict?
The weather department had made its first monsoon forecast on April 13, predicting that India is likely to witness 92 percent of LPA rainfall this monsoon season. However, during the IMD's second forecast for the southwest monsoon, it predicted less than 90 percent of LPA rainfall across the country. If the monsoon season sees less than 90 pc of LPA rainfall, the IMD classifies it as ‘deficient’.
Will El Nino affect southwest monsoon?
As per the IMD monsoon forecast, El Nino conditions are likely to be weak in June, and moderate to strong in September. Currently, neutral El Nino-Southern Oscillation conditions are transitioning towards El Nino conditions over the equatorial Pacific region. The emergence of El Nino conditions leads to less monsoon rainfall in the country.
